date.basis: announced November 2016, rolled out from April 2017 and completed by July 2017

documentedChange.note: The exact pre-reformulation figure is contested across the cited distinct sources: about 13g sugar per 100ml (The Grocer, Energy Orange) and about 17g carbohydrate per 100ml (diabetes.co.uk, Original); other national press reports gave about 12.4g sugar per 100ml but are not yet entered in the source registry. The singular figure is withheld while contested; both positions are carried below. No pre-2017 primary nutrition panel could be obtained, as the archival route was unavailable.

adjudication.note: Genuine, durable disagreement: the cited sources measure different quantities on different variants - about 13 g sugar per 100ml for Energy Orange (The Grocer) versus about 17 g carbohydrate per 100ml for Original (diabetes.co.uk) - not a transcription error; the single figure is withheld.

positions.value: About 13 g sugar per 100ml (Lucozade Energy Orange, pre-2017; reduced to 4.5 g)
positions.note: The Grocer trade-press figure for Energy Orange sugar.

positions.value: About 17 g carbohydrate per 100ml (Lucozade Original, pre-2017; reduced to 8.9 g)

statedReason.value: The world has changed with consumers now wanting healthier drinks and more action from the brands they regularly enjoy. (Peter Harding, Chief Operating Officer, Lucozade Ribena Suntory, November 2016)
statedReason.note: Attributed manufacturer statement. The characterisation of the change as levy-avoidance is press framing, not the manufacturer's own stated reason.

labelledInference.basis: The Soft Drinks Industry Levy took effect in April 2018 with a lower band at 5g sugar per 100ml; the reformulation to below 4.5g per 100ml completed in July 2017, ahead of the levy.
labelledInference.text: The target and timing are consistent with positioning the product below the levy's lower band, although the manufacturer's own stated reason emphasises consumer health rather than the levy.
